scope.notes: Shared weekly tasks are included explicitly. In the current code, they are cost-neutral in API-billing terms because they do not use paid model calls.

stages.reason: Topic selection, keyword targeting, cluster scoring, and content-plan generation are rule-based local operations with no model calls.

recent_feature_effects.reason: It reuses local cluster metadata and does not call a model.
Selection logic is local; only the article-generation prompt grew slightly.

stages.external_cost_source: Amazon PA-API or mock data (no direct usage fee modeled)
stages.reason: Amazon/mock product fetching uses external APIs or local JSON, but the repo does not indicate a direct per-call usage fee. Operationally important, but treated as $0 marginal cost here.

recent_feature_effects.feature: partial affiliate coverage and manual products file support
recent_feature_effects.reason: These change behavior and resilience, not direct API spend.

recent_feature_effects.feature: keyword targeting and topical cluster context
recent_feature_effects.reason: Adds prompt tokens to the same article-generation call, but does not add a new call.
Adds article body and JSON output size inside the same call.
Rendering is local; affiliate product metadata already exists inside the article package flow.

stages.reason: Structure, FAQ, product-link, and reading-time checks are local validation logic inside article generation.
Cannibalization, semantic coverage, and title checks are all rule-based local comparisons against JSON metadata.

recent_feature_effects.reason: No model call is used; it only compares strings and headings locally.

stages.reason: Publishing, internal-link surfacing, FAQ extraction, and affiliate-card HTML rendering are local file transformations.

recent_feature_effects.reason: Cards are rendered from existing metadata and markdown without extra API calls.
Schema is derived locally from article markdown.

assumptions.pricing_basis: Official square-image pricing scaled by pixel area for 1536x1024 output.

recent_feature_effects.reason: Pin templates render locally from the hero image and do not add OpenAI image calls.
This remains the largest direct cost driver because five section images are still generated per article.

assumptions.note: Image-input tokenization for vision QA is approximated as 3,000 input-token equivalents because the repo does not record exact billed image tokens.

recent_feature_effects.reason: Only the hero image still receives a QA model pass.

stages.reason: Pinterest title/description packaging, board selection, and strategy weighting are local logic.

recent_feature_effects.reason: Variant planning is local and uses saved analytics JSON only.

stages.reason: Branded pin templates are rendered locally as SVGs from the existing hero image; no new image-generation API calls occur.

recent_feature_effects.reason: This added local SVG rendering only.

stages.external_cost_source: Pinterest API (no direct usage fee modeled)
stages.reason: Pinterest publishing and analytics syncing use external APIs but no direct per-request usage fee is modeled in the repo.
The weekly newsletter draft is assembled from local article metadata, not generated by a model.

recent_feature_effects.reason: This is a local markdown assembly step.

stages.external_cost_source: Kit API (no direct usage fee modeled)
stages.reason: Kit broadcast draft creation is an external API operation but no direct per-call usage fee is modeled.
Cluster reports, hub/pillar pages, and article-cluster indexes are generated from local JSON metadata.

recent_feature_effects.reason: Static markdown pages are generated locally from the cluster index.

feature_impact_since_last_audit.reason: Slightly longer article prompts and article output, but still inside the single article-generation call.
Adds article body length and output JSON size to the same text-generation call.

feature_impact_since_last_audit.feature: cluster performance reports, content planning, hub pages, pillar pages
feature_impact_since_last_audit.reason: Generated from local metadata and markdown only.

feature_impact_since_last_audit.feature: newsletter generation and Kit draft creation
feature_impact_since_last_audit.reason: No model calls were added; the draft is assembled locally.

feature_impact_since_last_audit.feature: improved Pinterest pin generation and templates
feature_impact_since_last_audit.reason: Metadata and branded pin rendering stay local and reuse the hero image.
Only hero images still receive a QA model call.
Reruns can avoid most expensive model and image calls entirely.

previous_audit_reference.note: No prior cost audit file was found in the repo. This baseline comes from the earlier project estimate mentioned in the task context.

comparison_with_previous_audit.summary: Recent SEO, cluster, newsletter, and Pinterest template additions are mostly cost-neutral because they are local transformations. The main direct spend still comes from one text-generation call plus six image-generation calls.

optimization_recommendations.opportunity: Reduce section image count or make sections conditional on article type.
optimization_recommendations.reason: Five medium-quality section images remain the largest marginal cost driver.
optimization_recommendations.estimated_effect: Could reduce per-article cost by roughly $0.06 per skipped section image at current settings.

optimization_recommendations.opportunity: Generate fewer fresh article images for low-priority or experimental posts.
optimization_recommendations.reason: Image generation dominates total cost; text generation is comparatively cheap.
optimization_recommendations.estimated_effect: A hero-only mode would likely cut fresh-article cost by more than half.

optimization_recommendations.opportunity: Track actual token usage from OpenAI responses if the SDK exposes usage consistently for article generation.
optimization_recommendations.reason: Current text costs are estimated from prompt/package size, not billed token counts.
optimization_recommendations.estimated_effect: Would improve audit accuracy more than it would reduce spend.

optimization_recommendations.opportunity: Skip Pinterest analytics sync and summary rebuild on runs where no pins were published.
optimization_recommendations.reason: These stages are cost-neutral in API-billing terms but add runtime and external dependency churn.
optimization_recommendations.estimated_effect: Operational simplification rather than direct cost savings.

optimization_recommendations.opportunity: Bundle image QA signals into generation policy only if bad hero acceptance becomes an issue.
optimization_recommendations.reason: Hero QA cost is already very small after section QA removal.
optimization_recommendations.estimated_effect: Savings are marginal compared with image output costs.
